title: podman-v4.0.2.msi issues
body:
This may be me just having issues with Windows, but when running Podman on Windows after installing the 4.2 msi, I'm seeing 

A complaint about /tmpfs, which I wasn't able to make happy using the `--tmpfs` option.  I get the same error with or without the `--tmpfs`.

```
PS C:\Users\sween> podman run --tmpfs=mytmpfs ubi8-micro date
time="2022-03-05T19:51:45-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
Error: invalid container path "mytmpfs", must be an absolute path
```

Then when I try to use the `-d` option, the container runs until I try to exec into it and it appears to drop into a "Created" rather than "Up" state:

```
PS C:\Users\sween>  podman run -d --privileged --name myctr  -v mycontainers:/var/lib/containers quay.io/podman/stable:v3.3.1 sleep 600
time="2022-03-05T19:53:06-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
7760294587b7f17f930e95d7fa95c08b631f689ea442d3e48e7449277c3644b8

PS C:\Users\sween> podman ps
time="2022-03-05T19:53:15-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                         COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
7760294587b7  quay.io/podman/stable:v3.3.1  sleep 600   9 seconds ago  Up 9 seconds ago              myctr

PS C:\Users\sween> podman exec -it myctr /bin/sh
time="2022-03-05T19:53:42-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
Error: can only create exec sessions on running containers: container state improper

PS C:\Users\sween> podman ps
time="2022-03-05T19:53:47-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES

PS C:\Users\sween> podman ps -a
time="2022-03-05T19:55:21-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                         COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
7760294587b7  quay.io/podman/stable:v3.3.1                  sleep 600   2 minutes ago  Created                               myctr
```

Finally, this is almost definitely me, I could not find `containers.conf`, is there a location for it on Windows?

comments.body:
And fwiw, if I don't use the `-d` option and then open a new window, I can exec into the container without issue.

@n1hility @baude @ashley-cui any thoughts?

@TomSweeneyRedHat Sorry I have been meaning to but haven’t gotten around to fixing that tmp warning yet. It should be a small patch to common. The containers.conf is in your windows users home under AppData\Roaming\containers\containers.conf 

The other container state issue I had not run into yet. Does it happen when using —remote on Linux or Mac?

I also face the issue on Windows 11.  I wonder if is it effect to usage or not?   or just annoy message.

```
podman run busybox date
time="2022-03-06T11:34:08+07:00" level=warning msg="Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value \"/var/tmp\" (relative paths are not accepted)"
Sun Mar  6 04:34:08 UTC 2022
```
@peerasan it’s harmless just annoying. You should be able  override the config setting to make it go away.
@n1hilty I'm not able to get qemu to standup on my Fedora system, it could be user error.  I don't have a mac to test with.
and just to be sure, I'm using version 6.1.0 of qemu on Fedora, IDK if that might be part of the problem or not.
reopening.  The fix took care of the tmpdir issue, but the `podman run -d` not working is still present.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I briefly tried mac today with 4.0.2 and it did not happen there, so probably is win specific. I'll take a look and see what's going on

Finally got a chance to try it out and it works for me, although there is a tty warning I need to look into.
```
PS C:\Users\jason> podman exec -it myctr /bin/sh
time="2022-03-09T23:35:52-06:00" level=warning msg="Failed to obtain TTY size: The handle is invalid."
time="2022-03-09T23:35:53-06:00" level=warning msg="Failed to obtain TTY size: The handle is invalid."
sh-5.1#
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat can you do a podman version? I'm just wondering if you have a stale machine.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I have a local fix for the TTY issue, will send a PR tomorrow

@n1hility I had install 3.3* at one point, but when I went to install 4.0.2, I removed the Podman directory from the machine first and then install 4.0.2.  Perhaps leftover bits from the 3 install were still hanging around?

@TomSweeneyRedHat sorry I meant the wsl based podman machine instance, if there was any chance you had a podman 3 based linux on the backend that the windows remote was talking to.  If so it could be explained by a 4 to 3 api compat issue. If you are using the wsl machine, you should be able to just podman machine rm and podman machine init. Normally you would be able to do a podman machine ssh dnf update to pull down the latest but I only added the copr repo right before 4.0.1 (IIRC)
